Ridership/Performance Notes: All 5 planned zones launched by July 2024; Broad Rock added later for 6 total. Wheelchair accessible; ~20-min typical arrival. Serves areas without viable fixed-route coverage.

Name: CDTA FLEX+
Region/Service Area: Downtown Albany (Union Station/Amtrak to major downtown destinations)
Booking Method(s): Ride Pingo app, CDTA Navigator app, Phone
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched Jan 29, 2024; expanded summer 2024. Connects downtown Albany to Joseph L. Bruno Rail Station (one of busiest Amtrak stations). NY State IMI-funded ($1M allocation for CDTA).

Name: TriMet NEXT
Region/Service Area: South Gresham and Powell Valley neighborhoods, Gresham
Fleet: Not specified · Up to 6 riders per vehicle; WAV available
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched publicly June 2025 for seniors and people with disabilities. Connects to 79 bus lines, 5 MAX lines, WES commuter rail. Vehicles hold up to 6 riders. 100% rider satisfaction rating in first month. Earlier paratransit rollout April 2025.

Name: SPOT Southwest
Region/Service Area: South Cooper Mountain, River Terrace, Murrayhill, Progress Ridge (Washington County)
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched January 2026. Door-to-door shared rides; free to all community members. Connects to TriMet frequent bus at Washington Square Transit Center.

Name: High Valley Transit Micro
Region/Service Area: Multiple microzones in Summit and Wasatch counties (Park City area); discontinued in Park City municipal limits Apr 2024
Fleet: Not specified · WAV available; door-to-door service
Ridership/Performance Notes: 286,887 microtransit trips in 2023 alongside 894,598 fixed-route trips. ~35,000 monthly riders as of early 2025. Integrated with fixed-route buses; WAV and door-to-door service available. Service within Park City municipal limits ended Apr 14, 2024. [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 1,448,366 total trips across all modes, 44 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

Name: Decatur Moves
Region/Service Area: Decatur, Mt. Zion, Forsyth (urban zone); full Macon County (rural zone)
Operator: Decatur Public Transit System (DPTS); operated by MV Transportation
Funding Source: State (IDOT Consolidated Vehicle Program, downstate operating assistance), Local
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched Sept 8, 2025 after delay. 25-van fleet; 3-year $14M MV Transportation contract. Replaced no taxi service in Decatur since COVID. Citywide coverage replacing fixed-route bus gaps.

Name: TANK Plus
Fare: $5.00 curb-to-curb within zone; $2.50 to fixed-route transfer point
Funding Source: Federal, State, Local (Campbell County Fiscal Court)
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched Aug 13, 2024. On-demand only (no advance booking). Pickup within 30 minutes. Connects to TANK fixed routes for travel outside zone. Year-long pilot extended after success.

Name: MWRTA Catch Connect
Region/Service Area: Wellesley (all addresses); expanded to Hudson and Berlin; connections to Waban/Woodland MBTA stations, Newton Wellesley Hospital, Natick Community Center
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched Feb 2021 replacing Route 8 fixed bus in Wellesley. Expanded to Hudson/Berlin Feb 2025. Curb-to-curb; 10–30 min average wait. Part of broader MWRTA service area.

Name: Go2 (Muskegon Area Transit)
Ridership/Performance Notes: On-demand microtransit sponsored by MATS and operated by Via. Complements 7-route fixed-route network. Connects to Herman Ivory Terminal hub.

Name: Richmond Moves
Region/Service Area: City of Richmond (5.6 sq mi citywide zone; 2 BART stations, ferry terminal, Civic Center)
Funding Source: State (CA Clean Mobility Options Voucher Pilot $1M), Local
Fleet: 3 vehicles · Plug-in hybrid electric (WAV capable, up to 7 passengers)
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched April 2022. All-electric plug-in hybrid fleet. Serves predominantly low-income and disadvantaged communities (80% of service area). Connects to BART, Amtrak, AC Transit, and ferry.

Name: Calexico On Demand
Region/Service Area: City of Calexico (Imperial County, CA-Mexico border)
Funding Source: State (CA Clean Mobility Options Voucher $1M), Federal
Fleet: Not specified · Plug-in hybrid electric vehicles (PHEVs)
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched Jan 9, 2023. Plug-in hybrid electric vehicles. Serves seniors, low-income, and youth in a border city historically challenged by poor air quality. Contract extended through 2025 ($3M total). [NTD 2024: parent agency reported 977,774 total trips across all modes, 38 peak vehicles agency-wide; on-demand share is a subset]

Name: MetroConnect (Miami-Dade)
Region/Service Area: 10 zones across Miami-Dade County: Northern Miami-Dade, Northeastern, Westchester, Kendall, Dadeland, South Miami, West Perrine & South Miami Heights, Cutler Bay, Southern Miami-Dade (Civic Center and Waterford zones discontinued Feb 2026)
Fare: $3.75/trip; $1.50 discounted when connecting to Metrorail, Metrobus, or South Dade TransitWay
Service Hours: Mon–Fri 6:30am–7:00pm (South Miami: Thu–Sat noon–11pm, Sun noon–8:30pm)
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched 2020 as GoConnect; rebranded MetroConnect. 10+ zones across Miami-Dade. Feb 2026 restructuring standardized fares ($3.75) and discontinued 2 zones. One of largest public microtransit systems in Florida.

Name: ART On Demand (St. Lucie County)
Region/Service Area: 3 zones: SW Port St. Lucie (Zone 1), St. Lucie West/Torino (Zone 2), SW Fort Pierce/Treasure Coast Airport (Zone 3)
Operator: St. Lucie County Area Regional Transit (ART)
Booking Method(s): Rides on Demand app (rebranded Sept 2025), Phone
Ridership/Performance Notes: Won 2024 NACo Achievement Award (Best in Category, Transportation). 6,000+ trips/month. App upgraded to 'Rides on Demand' Sept 2, 2025. Inter-zone travel uses designated meet-up points.

Name: Freebee Miami Gardens
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched November 2025. Fleet growing from 6 to 10 vehicles by early 2026 due to strong demand. Part of Freebee's 53-destination Florida network.

Name: Freebee Palmetto Bay
Ridership/Performance Notes: 6,400+ rides in 2025. Fleet doubled to 4 vehicles Feb 2026. First/last-mile connectivity to South Dade TransitWay BRT corridor.

Name: Freebee Wynwood & Midtown
Region/Service Area: Wynwood Arts District, Midtown Miami, Edgewater; connection to Metromover at 15th St station
Ridership/Performance Notes: Operated by Wynwood BID. Connects Arts District to Edgewater, Midtown, Downtown/Brickell via Metromover. Fleet expanded to 4 VW ID vans Feb 2026.

Name: The HOP Microtransit (Hill Country Transit District)
Region/Service Area: 9 microtransit zones: Belton, Cameron, Copperas Cove, Gatesville, Harker Heights, Killeen, Lampasas, Salado, Temple (Bell, Coryell, Milam counties)
Booking Method(s): The HOP Rider App, Online, Phone (254-933-3700)
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched Sept 2024. Replaced multiple fixed routes with 36 microtransit vehicles across 9 zones. 46,000 boardings in Jan 2025 (highest month to date). Also offers Urban Regional Commuter (fixed schedule, Copperas Cove–Temple) and Rural Demand Response.

Name: Pierce Transit Runner
Region/Service Area: 5 zones in Pierce County: Spanaway North, Spanaway South, Puyallup, Tideflats, Ruston; plus Gig Harbor along Rt 16 corridor
Booking Method(s): Pierce Transit Runner app, Phone (253-581-8000 opt. 4)
Fare: Same as Pierce Transit bus ($2.00 adult, $1.00 reduced); Youth 18 and under free
Ridership/Performance Notes: 5+ zones across Pierce County. Via-powered. ORCA card, day pass, and app payment accepted. Zones adjusted June 2025 based on ridership data.

Name: Metro Link (Kalamazoo Metro)
Region/Service Area: Peripheral zones of Kalamazoo metro area (Comstock, eastern/western suburbs connecting to KMetro bus routes)
Operator: Kalamazoo Metro / Central County Transportation Authority (Via)
Fare: $1.50 (same as bus); senior discounts available; includes one bus transfer
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched April 15, 2024. Pilot through 2025; included in 2025 millage renewal. Extends reach of KMetro's 21-route bus network into lower-density areas. Via-powered corner-to-corner service.

Name: MicroCAT (Charlottesville Area Transit)
Region/Service Area: Pantops area and Route 29 North corridor, Albemarle County
Ridership/Performance Notes: Launched Oct 30, 2023. Extended beyond initial one-year pilot into 2025. Hybrid electric vehicles. Connects Pantops and Rt 29 N to Charlottesville/UVA fixed-route buses. 'Everything on the table' for expansion per CAT Director.
